passive.name: Deathbringer Stance
passive.description: Periodically, Aatrox's next basic attack deals bonus magic damage and heals him, based on the target's max health.

wiki_detail.description:
{{sbc|Innate:}} Periodically, '''Aatrox''' empowers his next [[basic attack]] to gain {{sti|range|50 '''bonus''' range}} and deal {{as|'''bonus''' magic damage}} equal to {{as|{{pp|key=%|4 to 8}} of the target's '''maximum''' health}}, capped at 100 against {{tip|monsters}}.
'''Aatrox''' {{tip|heals}} for 100% of the {{tt|post-mitigation '''bonus''' damage|Damage calculated after modifiers}} dealt, reduced to 25% against {{tip|minions}}.
wiki_detail.description2: Whenever '''Aatrox''' hits at least one enemy {{tip|champion}} or large {{tip|monster}} with a basic attack {{tip|on-hit}} or an [[champion ability|ability]], the {{sti|cooldown}} of ''Deathbringer Stance'' is reduced by 2 seconds, modified to 4 if he hits with the {{as|Sweetspot|sweetspot}} of ''{{ai|The Darkin Blade|Aatrox}}''.
wiki_detail.notes:
* '''Aatrox''' will assume stance when an enemy champion is in range of {{ai|Infernal Chains|Aatrox}}.
** The [[Emoting|toggle]] expression (default {{sbc|CTRL + 5}}) switches between stances manually.
* If ''Deathbringer Stance'' becomes available during a standard attack's windup, it will not be consumed or trigger the '''bonus''' damage.
* Even if the ability hit is {{tip|spell shield|spell shielded}} ''Deathbringer Stance's'' cooldown will still be reduced.
* This ability goes on cooldown on death and refreshes upon respawn.
* The empowered attack will not trigger against [[structures]] and [[ward]]s.
<!-- Blurb -->
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Innate:}} Periodically, {{cis|Aatrox}} next basic attack deals {{as|'''bonus''' magic damage}} based on the {{as|target's '''maximum''' health}}, {{as|{{sti|healing}}}} him for the damage dealt.
wiki_detail.blurb2: '''Aatrox's''' basic attacks and ability hits against enemy {{tip|champions}} or large {{tip|monsters}} {{sti|ah|reduce}} ''Deathbringer Stance's'' {{sti|cd|cooldown}}.

Q.name: The Darkin Blade
Q.description: Aatrox slams his greatsword down, dealing physical damage. He can swing three times, each with a different area of effect.
Q.tooltip: Aatrox slams his greatsword, dealing {{ qdamage }} physical damage. If they are hit on the edge, they are briefly Knocked Up and they take {{ qedgedamage }} instead. This Ability can be Recast twice, each one changing shape and dealing 25% more damage than the previous one.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.name: The Darkin Blade
wiki_detail.description: '''Aatrox''' can activate ''The Darkin Blade'' three times before the ability goes on cooldown, with a 1-second {{tt|static cooldown|Unaffected by ability haste}} between casts. If '''Aatrox''' does not recast the ability within 4 seconds of the previous cast, it goes on cooldown.
wiki_detail.description2: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Aatrox''' performs a strike with his greatsword for each of the three casts, dealing {{as|physical damage}} to enemies hit within an area. Enemies hit within a {{as|Sweetspot|sweetspot}} of the area take {{ccs|60% '''bonus''' damage|physical}} and also {{tip|airborne|knocked up}} for {{fd|0.25}} seconds. Each subsequent cast increases ''The Darkin Blade's'' damage by 25%.
wiki_detail.description3: {{sbc|First Cast:}} '''Aatrox's''' first strike affects a 625{{times}}180-unit rectangular area in the target direction, with him centered on the back line and the {{as|Sweetspot|sweetspot}} at the farthest edge.
wiki_detail.leveling3: {{st|First Cast Damage|{{ap|{{#var:b1}} to {{#var:b2}}}} {{as|(+ {{ap|{{#var:r1}} to {{#var:r2}}}}% AD)}}|First Sweetspot Damage|{{ap|{{#var:b1}}*1.6 to {{#var:b2}}*1.6}} {{as|(+ {{ap|{{#var:r1}}*1.6 to {{#var:r2}}*1.6}}% AD)}}}}
wiki_detail.description4: {{sbc|Second Cast:}} '''Aatrox's''' second strike affects a trapezoidal area in the target direction, with the {{as|Sweetspot|sweetspot}} at the farthest edge. The hitbox begins 100-units behind '''Aatrox''' and extends 475-units in front of him, measuring between 300 and 500-units wide from behind to in front.
wiki_detail.description5: {{sbc|Third Cast:}} '''Aatrox's''' third strike affects a 300-radius circular area centered on a target location that is 200 units in front of him, with a 180-radius {{as|Sweetspot|sweetspot}} within.
wiki_detail.description6: ''The Darkin Blade'' deals {{pp|55 to 70|key=%|1;6;11;16}} damage against {{tip|minions}}, and the {{tip|airborne|knock up}} duration from hitting the {{as|Sweetspot|sweetspot}} is doubled to {{fd|0.5}} seconds against {{tip|monsters}}.
wiki_detail.cooldown: {{tt|{{ap|14 to 6}}|Cooldown starts on first cast and restarts on each successive recast}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* [[File:Aatrox hitbox areas.png|thumb|The highlighted areas denote the sweetspot - enemies are affected where the two areas overlap (See {{tip|er|edge range}} and {{tip|cr|center range}}).]] The first and second casts function like any point-blank AOE (i.e. an effect centered on the caster). An enemy is considered to be hit by the ability based on {{tip|er|edge range}} - i.e. if any part of your {{tip|gameplay radius}} is within the hitbox, you are affected.
* The third cast, as well as the {{as|Sweetspot}} for the first and second cast, function like [[ground-targeted]] abilities. An enemy is considered to be hit by the ability based on {{tip|cr|center range}} - i.e. an enemy's center has to be within the hitbox to be affected.
* As implied by the previous point, the {{as|Sweetspot}} for the first and second cast is implemented as separate [[area of effect|areas of effect]] to the main component of the ability. Enemies must be within both areas to trigger the bonus damage, {{tip|airborne|knock up}} and {{ais|Deathbringer Stance|Aatrox}} cooldown reduction.
** The 'target gets hit' SFX plays whenever an enemy is within the {{as|Sweetspot}} - meaning it is possible to trigger the sound effect without affecting an enemy.
* Each cast counts as an ability activation for the purposes of on-cast effects such as {{tip|Spellblade}} and triggering {{ais|Force Pulse|Kassadin}} passive.
* The hitbox and '''Aatrox's''' model are fixed to the initial target direction.
** '''Aatrox's''' facing-direction, for effects such as {{ai|Petrifying Gaze|Cassiopeia}}, is the direction he is moving, and not the direction the model is facing.
* All damage modifiers {{tip|stacks multiplicatively|stack multiplicatively}}.
* There's a small period of time in which Aatrox can't declare basic attacks after casting ''The Darkin Blade''.{{bug|2}}
{{Effect at cast time end}}
{{ct|cast|cast= {{ai|E|Aatrox}} can be cast. Other abilities are disabled.|interrupts=death}}
wiki_detail.blurb: {{ci|Aatrox}} can cast this ability up to three times in a short amount of time before it goes on {{sti|cooldown}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2:
{{sbc|Active:}} '''Aatrox''' performs an attack with his greatsword in the target direction, dealing {{as|physical damage}} to enemies within an area. The area varies and the damage increases per cast. Enemies within each area's {{as|Sweetspot|sweetspot}} receive increased damage and are briefly {{tip|airborne|knocked up}}.
* [[File: The Darkin Blade.png|20px|link=]] {{sbc|First Cast:}} '''Aatrox''' strikes his greatsword in a long rectangle. 
* [[File: The Darkin Blade 2.png|20px|link=]] {{sbc|Second Cast:}} '''Aatrox''' slashes his greatsword in a wide trapezoid. 
* [[File: The Darkin Blade 3.png|20px|link=]] {{sbc|Third Cast:}} '''Aatrox''' slams his greatsword in a circle.

W.name: Infernal Chains
W.description: Aatrox smashes the ground, dealing damage to the first enemy hit. Champions and large monsters have to leave the impact area quickly or they will be dragged to the center and take the damage again.
W.tooltip: Aatrox fires a chain, Slowing the first enemy hit by {{ wslowpercentage*-100 }}% for {{ wslowduration }} seconds and dealing {{ wdamage }} physical damage. Champions and large jungle monsters have {{ wslowduration }} seconds to leave the impact area or be Pulled back to the center and damaged again for the same amount.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Aatrox''' sends a chain in the target direction that deals {{as|physical damage}} to the first enemy hit, doubled against {{tip|minions}}, and {{tip|slow|slowing}} them for {{fd|1.5}} seconds.
wiki_detail.leveling:
{{st|Physical Damage|{{ap|30 to 70}} {{as|(+ 40% AD)}}|Minion Damage|{{ap|30*2 to 70*2}} {{as|(+ 80% AD)}}}}
{{st|Slow|{{ap|25 to 35}}%}}
wiki_detail.description2: If this hits an enemy {{tip|champion}} or large {{tip|monster}}, a {{tip|tether}} is formed between the target and the ground beneath them for {{fd|1.5}} seconds, during which they are {{tip|true sight|revealed}}.
wiki_detail.description3: If the tether is not broken by the end of its duration, the target is dealt the same {{as|physical damage}} again and {{tip|airborne|pulled}} to the center of the area.
wiki_detail.notes:
* The impact area is oriented relative to '''Aatrox's''' position when the projectile hits, not where the projectile originated from.
* The location that the target is dragged to is not at the target's original location, but slightly closer towards '''Aatrox's''' position when the zone expires.
<!-- (Uncomment this note if this problem somehow still exists/comes back after V14.4)* There's a small delay in checking if the target is in or outside the ''Infernal Chains'' area. Because of this it is still possible to drag the target if they leave the zone at the last instant. -->
* {{tip|Spell shield}} will block the chain's application and initial damage but not the aftereffects of one already applied.
{{Effect at cast time end}}
wiki_detail.tips: * To succesfully escape when moving in a straight line away from '''Aatrox''' requires at least {{as|628 movement speed| movement speed}}.
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Aatrox}} sends a chain in the target direction that deals {{as|Physical damage}} and {{tip|slow|slows}} the first enemy hit.
wiki_detail.blurb2: If this hits an enemy {{tip|champion}} or large {{tip|monster}}, it creates a {{tip|true sight|revealing}} {{tip|tether|tether}} between them and the ground. If the tether is not broken after a brief moment, they are dealt {{as|physical damage}} and {{tip|airborne|pulled back}} to the center.
_comments: (Uncomment this note if this problem somehow still exists/comes back after V14.4)* There's a small delay in checking if the target is in or outside the ''Infernal Chains'' area. Because of this it is still possible to drag the target if they leave the zone at the last instant.

E.name: Umbral Dash
E.description: Passively, Aatrox heals when damaging enemy champions. On activation, he dashes in a direction.
E.tooltip:
Passive: Aatrox heals for {{ totalevamp }} of the damage he deals to Champions.

Active: Aatrox dashes. He can use this Ability while winding up his other Abilities.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Passive:}} '''Aatrox''' {{tip|heals}} for 16% {{as|(+ {{fd|0.9}}% per 100 '''bonus''' health)}} of non-{{tip|persistent damage|persistent}} {{tt|post-mitigation damage|Damage calculated after modifiers}} he deals against enemy champions.
wiki_detail.description3: ''Umbral Dash {{tip|basic attack reset|resets}} '''Aatrox's''' basic attack timer and can be cast during his other abilities without cancelling them and vice versa.''
wiki_detail.target range: {{tt|75|Minimum dash distance}} / {{tt|300|Maximum dash distance}} / {{tt|500|Maximum increased dash distance across terrain}}
wiki_detail.speed: {{tt|800|Slightly higher when measured}} / {{tt|Up to 1340|When dashing through thick terrain}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* The {{tip|dash}} distance can be extended to up-to 500 units when targeting across terrain. It will only extend for the distance needed to cross the wall.
** This may upscale his dash speed so that the dash takes the same total time as the standard maximum-range dash.
* If ''Umbral Dash'' is cast in the same direction '''Aatrox''' is moving, he will be unable to buffer commands that require him to move. {{bug|1}}
* '''Aatrox's''' model darkens for {{fd|1.5}} seconds upon casting ''Umbral Dash'', which is a remnant from a buff he received before it got removed.
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Passive:}} {{ci|Aatrox}} {{as|{{sti|heals}}}} for the portion of the damage he deals to champions.
wiki_detail.blurb4: ''Umbral Dash be cast during other abilities without cancelling them and vice versa.''

R.name: World Ender
R.description: Aatrox unleashes his demonic form, fearing nearby enemy minions and gaining attack damage, increased healing, and Move Speed. If he gets a takedown, this effect is extended.
R.tooltip:
Aatrox reveals his true demonic form, Fearing nearby minions for {{ rminionfearduration }} seconds and gaining {{ rmovementspeedbonus*100 }}% Move Speed decaying over {{ rduration }} seconds. He also gains {{ rtotaladamp*100 }}% Attack Damage and increases self-healing by {{ rhealingamp*100 }}% for the duration.

Champion takedowns extend the duration of this effect by {{ rextension }} seconds and refresh the Move Speed effect.{{ spellmodifierdescriptionappend }}

wiki_detail.description: {{sbc|Active:}} '''Aatrox''' unleashes his true form for 10 seconds, {{tip|fear|fearing}} nearby enemy {{tip|minions}} and {{tip|monsters}} for 3 seconds, during which they are gradually {{tip|slow|slowed}} by up to 99% over the duration. He also gains {{tip|ghosting}} and {{sti|{{as|'''bonus''' movement speed}}}} that decays by 10% of the '''current bonus''' every {{fd|0.25}} seconds, lasting until ''World Ender'' has ended.
wiki_detail.description2: Whenever '''Aatrox''' scores a champion {{tip|takedown}}, he extends the duration by 5 seconds and becomes unleashed again.
wiki_detail.description3: During ''World Ender'', '''Aatrox''' gains {{as|{{sti|'''bonus''' attack damage}}}} and 5% increased [[size]], and receives increased {{tip|heal|self-healing}} from all sources.
wiki_detail.leveling3:
{{st|Bonus Attack Damage|{{as|{{ap|20 to 40}}% AD}}}}
{{st|Increased Healing|{{ap|50 to 100}}%}}
wiki_detail.notes:
* His resource bar indicates the remaining duration of ''World Ender''.
* ''World Ender'' persists through {{tip|resurrection}} effects.
** ''World Ender's'' healing amplification applies to the health gained by resurrecting.
* ''World Ender'' will cast from wherever '''Aatrox''' is at the end of the cast time.
* Casting ''World Ender'' while it is still active renders '''Aatrox's''' wings invisible and removes the timer on the resource bar. {{bug}}
** This is possible via {{iis|Axiom Arc}} or [[Practice Tool|Practice Tool's]] cooldown reduction or by going on a very long killing spree.
wiki_detail.blurb: {{sbc|Active:}} {{ci|Aatrox}} unleashes his true form for a period, gaining a burst of {{sti|{{as|move speed}}}}. During this time, '''Aatrox''' has increased {{as|{{sti|attack damage}}}} and {{as|{{sti|self-healing}}}}.
wiki_detail.blurb2: These effects are refreshed and the duration is extended whenever '''Aatrox''' scores a champion {{tip|takedown}}.
